claims.evidence_text: An analysis of 9,686 comparisons between conservation agricultural practices and existing farmer practices in South Asia showed that zero tillage and residue retention offer a 5.8% yield advantage and a 25.9% economic advantage, with 12% to 33% less GHG emissions
claims.claim_summary: Zero tillage plus residue retention gives wheat-system ~5.8% yield advantage and 12-33% lower GHG vs conventional in South Asia.

claims.evidence_text: zero tillage and residue retention offer a 5.8% yield advantage and a 25.9% economic advantage, with 12% to 33% less GHG emissions, compared to existing practices
claims.claim_summary: Crop residue retention (with zero tillage) improves wheat-system yield ~5.8% and economics ~25.9% in South Asia.
